title: Lemongrass Braised Crisp Dish
description: A light and peppery Brazilian dish featuring lemongrass stalks, braise to perfection with a dash-friendly twist.
instructions: Rinse lemongrass and trim off the tough outer layer. Mince garlic finely.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic to the hot oil, sautéing until fragrant and slightly golden, about 2 minutes.
Gently add chicken pieces to the pot, skin side down first if using. Brown on both sides for about 5 minutes each.
Remove chicken from the pot and set aside. Add lemongrass stalks and red chilis (if using) to the same pot. Stir-fry until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Pour in water, scraping all the browned bits off the bottom of the pot to enhance the umami flavor. Bring to a boil.

Return chicken pieces to the pot with lemongrass mixture. Add salt and pepper to taste. Reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for about 20-30 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and falls apart easily.
Remove from heat and serve hot.
